description_long: This is a rustic wooden stool with a round, flat seat and four straight legs that taper slightly as they extend downward. The wood grain is clearly visible, giving the stool a weathered and natural appearance, with some surface discoloration and marks that suggest frequent use. The legs are connected by two sets of horizontal support bars, adding stability and strength to the structure. The stool lacks any backrest or armrest, emphasizing its simple and utilitarian design. There are no visible decorative elements or embellishments, and the finish is matte, highlighting the raw texture of the wood. This type of stool is commonly found in public spaces such as parks, bus stops, or outdoor seating areas in urban environments.
description: A rustic, round wooden stool with four straight legs and visible wood grain, featuring horizontal support bars and a matte, weathered finish.
description_view_0: The stool is seen from the front, showing the round seat, four legs, and two horizontal support bars, with the wood grain and weathering clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the thickness of the seat and the alignment of the legs, with the support bars partially visible.
description_view_2: Another angled view of the stool, similar to view 1, showing the round seat and the sturdy leg construction.
description_view_3: The back view of the stool, nearly identical to the front, with the same leg and support bar arrangement and visible wood texture.
